parameters.description: PEM-encoded X.509 certificate bundle added to nodes' trust store; used when proxy is configured.

parameters.description: Custom feature gate configuration for enabling/disabling specific cluster features. Only valid when featureSet is 'CustomNoUpgrade'. Each entry is 'FeatureGateName=true' to enable or 'FeatureGateName=false' to disable. Example: ['ExperimentalFeature=true', 'OldFeature=false']. Clusters with custom feature gates cannot upgrade.
Feature set to enable (features not enabled by default).

parameters.description: MTU for cluster network (pod-to-pod traffic). Defaults to 8951 for OVN-Kubernetes (Geneve overhead), 1450 for OpenShiftSDN.

parameters.description: IP address blocks for machines. Must match CIDR of preferred NIC.

IPv4 join subnet used internally by ovn-kubernetes. Must not overlap node network. Cannot change after install.

citations.docTitle: Installation configuration parameters for the Agent-based Installer

parameters.description: IP address block for services. OVNKubernetes supports single block or dual-stack.

parameters.description: Configuration for the platform for installation.

citations.docTitle: Installation configuration parameters for the Agent-based Installer

parameters.allowed: vsphere.Platform object with vCenter, datacenter, cluster, network, etc.
parameters.description: vSphere platform configuration for installer-provisioned infrastructure. Includes vCenter connection details, datacenter, cluster, datastore, network, folder paths. Required when platform.type is 'vsphere'.

parameters.allowed: HTTP or HTTPS URL, optionally with SHA-256 checksum
parameters.description: Optional machine pool: URL for RHCOS image. Must match release boot image version. 9.1.6. Choose one: this OR failureDomains[].topology.template.

conditionals.visibility: Optional; one of two RHCOS image methods
conditionals.requiredness: If used, must be exclusive of topology.template
conditionals.narrative: Choose one method for RHCOS image: clusterOSImage URL OR topology.template path (pre-imported OVA in vCenter).

citations.sectionHeading: 9.1.6. Optional VMware vSphere machine pool configuration parameters

parameters.allowed: integer; cpus / coresPerSocket = number of sockets
parameters.description: Optional machine pool: cores per socket. Control plane and compute default to 4 virtual sockets. 9.1.6.

citations.sectionHeading: 9.1.6. Optional VMware vSphere machine pool configuration parameters

parameters.allowed: integer; must be multiple of coresPerSocket
parameters.description: Optional machine pool: total virtual processor cores per VM. 9.1.6.

citations.sectionHeading: 9.1.6. Optional VMware vSphere machine pool configuration parameters

parameters.description: vSphere datacenter name. Deprecated in 4.13+; prefer failureDomains[].topology.datacenter and vcenters[].datacenters; still supported.

citations.docTitle: Installing a cluster on vSphere with user-provisioned infrastructure

parameters.description: Default datastore for cluster VMs. Deprecated in 4.13+; prefer failureDomains[].topology.datastore; still supported for legacy single-placement path.
Optional disk provisioning method. Defaults to vSphere storage policy if not set. thin, thick, or eagerZeroedThick.

parameters.allowed: failure domain objects with name, region, zone, server, topology
parameters.description: Establishes relationships between region and zone. Defines vCenter location for cluster nodes. Array allows multiple failure domains.

vSphere tag category type for region definition. Defines how regions are mapped in vSphere infrastructure. Datacenter = use datacenters as regions, ComputeCluster = use compute clusters, HostGroup = use host groups. Used with vSphere zones for failure domain configuration.

parameters.allowed: FailureDomainTopology object with computeCluster, datacenter, datastore, networks, resourcePool
parameters.description: vSphere topology configuration for failure domain. Defines vSphere infrastructure resources for a specific failure domain: compute cluster, datacenter, datastore, networks, resource pool. Used for multi-zone vSphere deployments.

vSphere tag category type for zone definition. Defines how zones are mapped in vSphere infrastructure. Datacenter = use datacenters as zones, ComputeCluster = use compute clusters, HostGroup = use host groups. Used with vSphere regions for failure domain configuration.
The name of the failure domain.
For multiple failure domains use openshift-region tag; for single DC use an alphanumeric value e.g. datacenter.
FQDN or IP of the vCenter server for this failure domain.
The path to the vSphere compute cluster.
Datacenter where cluster VMs operate. Must match vcenters datacenters.
Path to the vSphere datastore for this failure domain.
Optional absolute path of folder for VMs e.g. /datacenter/vm/folder.
Networks in vCenter that contain the virtual IP addresses and DNS records.
Optional absolute path of resource pool for VMs e.g. /datacenter/host/cluster/Resources/pool.
For multiple failure domains use openshift-zone tag; for single DC use an alphanumeric value e.g. cluster.

parameters.allowed: absolute VM folder path in vSphere inventory
parameters.description: VM folder path in vSphere inventory (e.g., /datacenter/vm/folder). Deprecated in 4.13+; prefer failureDomains[].topology.folder; still supported for legacy single placement.

conditionals.note: Only used when placementMode is legacy; ignored when failureDomains are defined

parameters.description: Optional machine pool: VM memory in megabytes. 9.1.6.

citations.sectionHeading: 9.1.6. Optional VMware vSphere machine pool configuration parameters

parameters.description: Legacy single-zone network name. Use failureDomains[].topology.network for multi-zone.
Optional machine pool: OS disk size in gigabytes. 9.1.6.

citations.sectionHeading: 9.1.6. Optional VMware vSphere machine pool configuration parameters

parameters.allowed: absolute resource pool path in vSphere inventory
parameters.default: root resource pool of the cluster
parameters.description: Resource pool path in vSphere inventory (e.g., /datacenter/host/cluster/Resources/pool). Deprecated in 4.13+; prefer failureDomains[].topology.resourcePool; still supported for legacy single placement.

conditionals.note: Only used when placementMode is legacy; ignored when failureDomains are defined

parameters.description: vCenter server FQDN or IP. Deprecated in 4.13+; prefer vcenters[].server; still supported.

citations.docTitle: Installing a cluster on vSphere with user-provisioned infrastructure

parameters.allowed: vCenter connection objects with server, user, password, port, datacenters
parameters.description: Connection details for vCenter server(s). Datacenters must match failureDomains.
Datacenters where cluster VMs operate. Must match failureDomains topology.datacenter.
vCenter password. Emitted when including credentials.
Port for vCenter server. Default 443.
FQDN or IP address of the vCenter server.
vCenter username. Emitted when including credentials.

parameters.allowed: Proxy object with httpProxy, httpsProxy, noProxy fields
parameters.description: Cluster-wide proxy configuration for external HTTP/HTTPS connections. Configure if cluster nodes access Red Hat services, container registries, or external resources through a proxy server. If unset, cluster does not use a proxy.
Proxy URL for HTTP connections outside the cluster. URL scheme must be http.

citations.sectionHeading: 1.11.2. Configuring the cluster-wide proxy during installation

parameters.description: Proxy URL for HTTPS connections outside the cluster.

citations.sectionHeading: 1.11.2. Configuring the cluster-wide proxy during installation

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of destinations to exclude from proxying. Prefix domain with . for subdomains only; use * to bypass proxy for all.

citations.sectionHeading: 1.11.2. Configuring the cluster-wide proxy during installation

parameters.description: How to publish cluster endpoints (Kubernetes API, routes). Internal not supported on non-cloud.

parameters.description: Pull secret from Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager to authenticate image pulls from Quay.io etc.

parameters.description: SSH key to authenticate access to cluster machines. Recommend for production.
